q1: What is your overall experience with tennis and other racket sports?
q1a: I have never played tennis or any other racket sport.
q1b: I have a background in padel or another racket sport, but I'm brand new to tennis.
q1c: I've played tennis a few times and know the very basics.
q1d: I play tennis regularly and feel comfortable on the court.
q2: How easily can you keep a rally going from the baseline?
q2a: I struggle to hit the ball over the net or keep it in bounds.
q2b: I can keep a slow-paced rally going, but I struggle with my backhand or faster balls.
q2c: I am consistent on medium-paced shots, both forehand and backhand.
q2d: I can comfortably control the direction, depth, and speed of my groundstrokes during a rally.
q3: How confident are you with your serve and return?
q3a: I'm still learning the basic serve motion and rarely get serves in.
q3b: I can get slow serves in and mostly block returns back into play.
q3c: I have a reliable first serve at a moderate pace and return average serves comfortably.
q3d: I use serve placement and spin and confidently return to start points on my terms.
q4: How comfortable are you with net play and transitioning forward?
q4a: I prefer to stay on the baseline; the net feels intimidating.
q4b: I come forward occasionally and can hit basic volleys if the ball comes to me.
q4c: I move forward with intent and hit approach shots and basic volleys with confidence.
q4d: I play aggressively at the net and am comfortable with volleys, overheads, and transitions.
q5: How would you describe your match play and knowledge of tennis rules?
q5a: I'm still learning scoring, lets, and where to stand in doubles.
q5b: I know basic rules and positioning, but I mostly react without much of a plan.
q5c: I understand doubles positioning, serving order, and common match tactics.
q5d: I'm comfortable with match format, tie-breaks, and adjusting strategy during doubles play.
